message:
perf(showcase): cache markdown render across width-stable redraws

The Markdown / Rich Text demo screen was re-running the full
`MarkdownRenderer` pipeline on every frame for both the static panel
and the streaming-fragment panel. The renderer parses the markdown,
drives `pulldown-cmark` events, runs the syntax highlighter on each
fenced code block, and synthesizes themed `Text<'static>` lines —
work that's heavy enough to show up in the showcase frame budget at
60 FPS even at modest fragment sizes, and that produces an identical
output every frame as long as the panel's width and the fragment
content are unchanged.

This commit adds two width-keyed rendered-text caches and threads
them through the rendering path:

== Static-panel cache: `RenderedMarkdownCache` ==

Wraps `Option<Text<'static>>` plus the `Option<u16>` width that
produced it. `text(width, renderer, markdown)` invalidates the cache
on width change (so a resized terminal still rerenders correctly),
and otherwise returns a borrow into the cached Text. The renderer
clone is intentional — `MarkdownRenderer` is `Clone` and the original
borrow lives behind an `&'a` that we can't extend, so cloning the
renderer for the one-time cache fill is the cheapest way to hand the
result back as `&Text<'static>` to the Paragraph.

`MarkdownPanel` no longer carries the per-frame `table_phase` field
(table theme phasing was already inert in the static panel — only
the streaming path animates) and instead carries `render_cache:
&RefCell<RenderedMarkdownCache>` so the parent screen owns the
backing storage and the panel borrows it for the frame.

== Streaming-panel cache: `StreamRenderCache` ==

Slightly more involved because the streaming fragment also drives
the detection-status display via `is_likely_markdown(fragment)`.
Both calls were running every frame against the same fragment. The
cache key is `(width, stream_position, stream_complete)` so the
renderer (and the detection scan) only re-runs when the visible
prefix actually grows, when the user toggles complete-vs-streaming,
or when the panel resizes. `text_and_detection(...)` returns
`(&Text<'static>, MarkdownDetection)` so the detection-status panel
shares the same per-fragment work the renderer just paid for.

Also removes the now-redundant `is_likely_markdown(fragment)` call
that ran AFTER the renderer had already produced its output — the
detection answer comes back from the cache lookup instead, so the
caller saves one full markdown scan per frame on the streaming path.

== Cache-clear hooks ==

The reset key (`'r'` rewinding the stream) explicitly clears the
streaming cache via `self.stream_render_cache.borrow_mut().clear()`
so a rewound stream re-renders against the new (empty) prefix
instead of returning the cached complete-fragment text. Width
changes are handled passively by the `width != Some(width)`
invalidation inside both caches' `text(...)` paths.

== Why a `RefCell` ==

The render functions take `&self` (Screen trait constraint), so the
cache fill needs interior mutability. The cache is touched only on
the render thread, so the `RefCell` is never racing — borrow_mut on
a single-threaded path is fine. `MarkdownPanel` borrows the cell as
`&RefCell<...>` so the outer screen retains ownership.

No public API changes; this is a purely internal optimization in the
showcase demo. Visible behavior is identical except for the lower
per-frame CPU cost.
